turns.content: Set up a new TypeScript project the way I like it

     8â†’technology choices across all development work - languages, package managers,
     9â†’formats, and workflow patterns.
    10â†’
    11â†’LOCATION:
    12â†’- Kai (Private): ${PAI_DIR}/skills/CORE/USER/TECHSTACKPREFERENCES.md
    13â†’- PAI Pack: Packs/pai-core-install/src/skills/CORE/USER/TECHSTACKPREFERENCES.md
    14â†’
    15â†’CUSTOMIZATION:
    16â†’- [ ] Adjust language preferences to match your stack
    17â†’- [ ] Update package manager preferences
    18â†’- [ ] Modify format standards as needed
    19â†’- [ ] Add your own workflow patterns
    20â†’
    21â†’RELATED FILES:
    22â†’- SYSTEM/TOOLS.md - CLI utilities reference
    23â†’- SYSTEM/CLIFIRSTARCHITECTURE.md - CLI-first design patterns
    24â†’- ALGOPREFS.md - AI behavior customizations
    25â†’
    26â†’LAST UPDATED: 2026-01-08
    27â†’VERSION: 1.1.0
    28â†’================================================================================
    29â†’-->
    30â†’
    31â†’# Stack Preferences
    32â†’
    33â†’**Your Technology Stack Preferences - Definitive Reference**
    34â†’
    35â†’This document captures your core technology stack preferences for PAI infrastructure and all development work.
    36â†’
    37â†’---
    38â†’
    39â†’## Languages
    40â†’
    41â†’### Primary Language: Python
    42â†’
    43â†’**Primary Rule:** Use Python for data science, analysis, and general scripting.
    44â†’
    45â†’**When to Use Python:**
    46â†’- All new infrastructure development
    47â†’- Data analysis and machine learning
    48â†’- Command-line tools and utilities
    49â†’- Default choice for all new projects
    50â†’
    51â†’**When TypeScript/Other is Acceptable:**
    52â†’- Web development / Frontend
    53â†’- Config files requiring JSON/YAML/JS
    54â†’
    55â†’---
    56â†’
    57â†’## Package Managers
    58â†’
    59â†’### Python: pip
    60â†’
    61â†’**Commands:**
    62â†’```bash
    63â†’# Install packages
    64â†’pip install package-name
    65â†’
    66â†’# Requirements
    67â†’pip freeze > requirements.txt
    68â†’pip install -r requirements.txt
    69â†’```
    70â†’
    71â†’### JavaScript/TypeScript: npm/bun
    72â†’
    73â†’**Commands:**
    74â†’```bash
    75â†’# Install dependencies
    76â†’npm install
    77â†’
    78â†’# Add a new package
    79â†’npm install package-name
    80â†’```
    81â†’
    82â†’---
    83â†’
    84â†’## Formats & Standards
    85â†’
    86â†’### Documentation Format: Markdown
    87â†’
    88â†’**Primary Rule:** Use Markdown for all documentation.
    89â†’
    90â†’**Acceptable HTML:**
    91â†’- Custom components (`<aside>`, `<details>`, `<summary>`)
    92â†’- Interactive elements requiring specific behavior
    93â†’
    94â†’**Avoid HTML for:**
    95â†’- Basic paragraphs, headers, lists
    96â†’- Links and emphasis
    97â†’- Code blocks and tables
    98â†’
    99â†’---
   100â†’
   101â†’## Workflow Patterns
   102â†’
   103â†’### Git Practices
   104â†’
   105â†’**Style:** Commit often with simple descriptions and merge when branch work is done.
   106â†’
   107â†’**Commit Messages:**
   108â†’- Simple and descriptive
   109â†’- Example: "update user contact info" or "fix data processing bug"
   110â†’- Merge strategy: Merge branch when work is complete
   111â†’
   112â†’### Analysis vs Action: Explicit Intent Required
   113â†’
   114â†’**Analysis Tasks (Read-Only):**
   115â†’- "Analyze the authentication flow"
   116â†’- "Review this code for issues"
   117â†’- "What's wrong with this implementation?"
   118â†’
   119â†’**Action Tasks (Modifications Allowed):**
   120â†’- "Fix the authentication bug"
   121â†’- "Refactor this code"
   122â†’- "Implement the new feature"
   123â†’
   124â†’**Rule:** If asked to analyze, do analysis ONLY - don't change things unless explicitly asked.
   125â†’
   126â†’---
   127â†’
   128â†’## Terminal & Browser
   129â†’
   130â†’### Terminal: Terminal.app
   131â†’
   132â†’Use the standard macOS Terminal.
   133â†’
   134â†’### Browser: Google Chrome
   135â†’
   136â†’```bash
   137â†’# Open a URL
   138â†’open -a "Google Chrome" "http://localhost:5200"
   139â†’```
   140â†’
   141â†’---
   142â†’
   143â†’## Summary Reference Card
   144â†’
   145â†’```
   146â†’LANGUAGES:
   147â†’  Primary: Python
   148â†’  Secondary: TypeScript
   149â†’
   150â†’PACKAGE MANAGERS:
   151â†’  JS/TS: Bun
   152â†’  Python: Pip
   153â†’
   154â†’FORMATS:
   155â†’  Documentation: Markdown
   156â†’  Config: JSON/YAML
   157â†’
   158â†’WORKFLOW:
   159â†’  Analysis â†’ Read only, report findings
   160â†’  Action â†’ Modify with confidence
   161â†’```
   162â†’
   163â†’---
   164â†’
   165â†’**This is the definitive reference for stack preferences. When in doubt, consult this document.**
   166â†’
